title: malus orientalis subsp. montana
intro: Malus orientalis subsp. montana, also known as the Caucasian mountain apple, is a subspecies of apple native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malus orientalis subsp. montana include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns, such as colds and respiratory issues.
